recalls.Description: VESTA.DS has announced a recall of VST-brand residential wall-mounted gas boilers, specifically the Series VT, VR, and VR Plus. The affected models include VRS-150, VRS-199, VRP-150, VRP-199, VRS Plus-150, VRS Plus-199, VRP Plus-150, VRP Plus-199, VTS-150, VTS-199, VTP-150, and VTP-199. These metal, rectangular units feature a label on the side with the model number prominently displayed. The control panel may have 'VST' in white or 'VESTA.DS.Inc.' in gray printed on it.
recalls.Title: VESTA.DS Issues Recall for VST Brand Tankless Water Heaters Due to Carbon Monoxide Poisoning Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact VESTA.DS at 888-505-5525 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at condensingwaterheater@realtimeresults.net or visit their website at www.recallrtr.com/condensingwaterheater or https://www.vestahws.com and select 'Recall' under the 'Documents/Support' tab for further details.

Products.Name: VST Brand Tankless Water Heaters
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 36,700 (In addition, about 3,500 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled VR Plus and VT Series Vesta Water Heaters
Recalled VR Series Vesta Water Heater
Model Number on the Label of the Recalled Water Heaters

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 33 reports of cracked exhaust ducts allowing exhaust fumes to leak out. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available in-store and online at Best Buy, Dick's Sporting Goods, Nebraska Furniture Mart, and ABT, as well as online at nordictrack.com and Amazon.com from November 2018 through April 2022, priced around $1,700.

Hazards.Name: Risk of carbon monoxide (CO) poisoning due to exhaust duct cracking

Remedies.Name: Free repair installation by a certified technician; ensure working smoke and CO alarms if continuing use

recalls.metaDescription: VESTA.DS recalls VST tankless water heaters due to carbon monoxide risks. Learn how to get your unit repaired safely.

recalls.Description: The recall pertains to VFAUOSIT kitchen faucets, specifically model number 06BNV. These brushed nickel, single-handle faucets feature a pull-down detachable spray and are designed for single-hole installation. To determine if your faucet is part of this recall, check your Amazon purchase history for 'Vfauosit' and 'kitchen faucet,' looking for descriptions that mention features such as a high-arc design and pull-out functionality for various sink types.
recalls.Title: VFAUOSIT Kitchen Faucets Recalled for Lead Exposure Risk to Young Children; Available on Amazon.com by Whisper08
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, reach out to Whisper08 via email at customerservice@vfauosit.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled VFAUOSIT Kitchen Faucet

Manufacturers.Name: Jiang men cai nuo wei yu you xian gong si, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Whisper08

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com from January 2024 through May 2025 for approximately $40.

Hazards.Name: Lead contamination that can leach into water, posing serious health risks to infants and young children, including neurological issues and cognitive impairments.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the faucets immediately and contact Whisper08 for guidance on disposal and obtaining a refund. A photo of the disposed faucet along with the order number will be required for the refund process. Whisper08 will also reach out to known purchasers directly. Until replacements are provided, it is advised to run the faucet for 15 seconds before using the water.

recalls.metaDescription: VFAUOSIT kitchen faucets recalled due to lead exposure risks. Contact Whisper08 for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: KICIMPRO has issued a recall for their kitchen faucets, specifically model KM800-01-06BN. These brushed nickel, single-handle faucets feature a pull-down spray and are designed for single-hole installation. Consumers can check their Amazon purchase history for the terms 'Kicimpro' and 'kitchen faucet' to identify affected products, which are described as high-arc, commercial modern RV stainless steel faucets with water lines.
recalls.Title: KICIMPRO Kitchen Faucets Recalled Due to Lead Exposure Risk for Children
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Kicimpro via email at support@kicimprohome.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled KICIMPRO Kitchen Faucet

Manufacturers.Name: Kaipingshikemuyulinweiyushiyeyouxiangongsi, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Kicimpro

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com from January 2024 to May 2025, priced around $35.

Hazards.Name: Potential lead contamination in water, posing serious health risks to infants and young children.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the faucets immediately and reach out to Kicimpro for disposal instructions and a refund. A photo of the disposed faucet and the order number will be required for the refund process. Kicimpro will also contact all known buyers directly. Until replacements are available, run the water for 15 seconds before consumption.

recalls.metaDescription: KICIMPRO recalls kitchen faucets due to lead exposure risks. Check your purchase history and contact support for refunds.

recalls.Description: BASDEHEN kitchen faucets, specifically model CFDTTH-000-YGH, are being recalled due to dangerous levels of lead that can leach into water, posing a significant health risk to young children. These faucets are characterized by a matte black finish, a single-handle design, 360° swivel, a spring spout, a detachable spray feature, and an optional mounting plate for single-hole installation. Consumers can check their Amazon purchase history for the terms "Basdehen" and "kitchen faucet" to determine if they own a recalled model.
recalls.Title: Recall of BASDEHEN Kitchen Faucets Due to Lead Contamination Risk for Children; Available on Amazon.com
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Chenfeng Store via email at ChenfengStoreF@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: Recalled BASDEHEN Kitchen Faucet

Manufacturers.Name: Ruianshichenfengwujinzhizaoyouxiangongsi, of China, doing business as Amazon seller Chenfeng Store

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.amazon.com from May 2024 to May 2025, priced around $30.

Hazards.Name: Lead contamination and other harmful contaminants found in the faucets can leach into water, posing serious health risks to infants and young children.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must stop using the faucets immediately and reach out to Chenfeng Store for instructions on disposal and obtaining a refund. A photo of the disposed faucet is required for the refund process. Amazon is proactively notifying all known buyers. Until replacements are available, it is advised to run the water for 15 seconds before consumption.

recalls.metaDescription: BASDEHEN kitchen faucets recalled due to lead exposure risks for children. Check your purchase history and contact for a refund.

recalls.Description: Liberty Hardware has issued a recall for Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars sold under the Peerless, Safety First, and Delta brands. The affected models include Peerless PL585, Safety First SF585 and S1F585, and Delta DF585. These safety bars, made from powder-coated aluminum and available in white, feature a one-inch diameter tube with gripping surfaces located approximately 8 inches and 15 inches apart. The model number and brand are indicated on the product packaging label.
recalls.Title: Liberty Hardware Recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars Due to Fall Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: Consumers should stop using the recalled Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars immediately and reach out to Liberty Hardware at 844-811-4541 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. They can also email recall@libertyhardware.com or visit www.libertyhardware.com/recall-notices/tub-safety-bar for more information.

Images.Caption: Recalled Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bar

Injuries.Name: Liberty Hardware has received fourteen reports of the plastic sticker grip pads becoming dislodged after installation or otherwise needing replacement, including one fall with a minor injury reported.

Importers.Name: Liberty Hardware Manufacturing Corp., of Winston-Salem, North Carolina

Hazards.Name: Risk of falls due to dislodged grip pads

Remedies.Name: Request a prepaid label and packaging for a $35 refund

recalls.metaDescription: Liberty Hardware recalls Multi-Grip Tub Safety Bars due to fall hazards. Learn how to get a refund and check if your model is affected.

recalls.Description: Approximately 866,000 AirJet and HydroJet Spa Pumps are being recalled due to a significant fire hazard. These pumps may have been sold individually or bundled with inflatable spa liners in various colors and styles. The liners are branded as SaluSpa, Coleman, and Hydro-Force, clearly labeled on the side. The recall specifically affects model numbers P05332, P05339, P05711, P05807, P07000, P07001, P05511, P07034, and P07572, with 'Bestway' and the model number found on the pump's label.
recalls.Title: Recall of 866,000 AirJet and HydroJet Spa Pumps by Bestway Due to Fire Risk; One Fatal Incident Reported
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Bestway at 888-943-2396 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, or visit www.bestwayusa.com/product-safety-and-recall for more details.

Images.Caption: Recalled AirJet Spa Pump
Recalled HydroJet Spa Pump
Recalled AirJet Spa Pump model number located on label on side of unit
Recalled HydroJet Spa Pump model number located on label on back of unit
Recalled Spa Pump attached to inflatable spa liner

Injuries.Name: CPSC is aware of at least three fires reportedly involving the spa pumps, including one house fire that occurred in January 2024 in Kansas City, Missouri, which resulted in a fatality, and one additional fire that resulted in significant property damage. No additional injuries have been reported. There are seven additional incidents reportedly involving the spa pumps, which are currently under investigation. At this time no injuries have been reported with these additional incidents.

Manufacturers.Name: Bestway (Hong Kong) International Ltd., of China

Retailers.Name: These spa pumps were available for purchase alongside spa liners at Bestway USA, Amazon, Costco, Walmart, Sam's Club, QVC, RuralKing, Spreetail, and various other retailers across the country from May 2021 to May 2024, priced between $400 and $790.

Hazards.Name: Overheating leading to ignition of the plastic housing, creating a fire risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the pumps immediately and contact Bestway for a free replacement or a refund of $100 for AirJet pumps and $189 for HydroJet pumps.

recalls.metaDescription: Bestway recalls 866,000 AirJet and HydroJet Spa Pumps due to fire hazards. One death reported. Contact Bestway for refunds or replacements.

recalls.Description: The recall affects specific models of Camplux portable tankless water heaters, including AY132, BD158, BW211, BW264, and BW422, produced on or before various dates in late 2021. These outdoor water heaters, designed for quick heating with an attached propane tank, are available in white, black, and silver/gray. Each unit features the 'Camplux' logo and has a product label on the side detailing model numbers and other specifications. Consumers are urged to check their units for the recall label to verify if their product is affected.
recalls.Title: Camplux Portable Tankless Water Heaters Recalled Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact The Home Appliances ("Camplux") at 844-538-7475, available from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also reach them via email at support@camplux.com or visit www.thehomeappliances.com and click on 'Recalls' or go directly to www.thehomeappliances.com/waterheaterrecall for additional information.

Products.Name: Camplux AY132 Portable Tankless Water Heater

Images.Caption: Recalled Camplux brand AY132 portable tankless water heater – white
Recalled Camplux brand AY132 portable tankless water heater – black
Recalled Camplux brand AY132 portable tankless water heater – gray/silver
Recalled Camplux brand BD158 portable tankless water heater
Recalled Camplux brand BW211 portable tankless water heater – white
Recalled Camplux brand BW211 portable tankless water heater – gray/silver
Recalled Camplux brand BW264 portable tankless water heater
Recalled Camplux brand BW422 portable tankless water heater

Injuries.Name: Camplux has received 19 reports of fire incidents involving the recalled water heaters. No injuries have been reported.

Importers.Name: The Home Appliances Inc., d/b/a Camplux, of North Charleston, South Carolina

Hazards.Name: Gas leakage from the regulator connection leading to fire hazards

Remedies.Name: Consumers should stop using the recalled water heaters immediately and contact The Home Appliances for a free repair kit, which includes a replacement gas regulator with a quick connect feature and installation instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Camplux recalls portable tankless water heaters due to fire risks. Check if your model is affected and learn how to get a free repair kit.

recalls.Description: Steamist is recalling its wall-mounted, folding Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats, which are constructed from teak wood slats supported by rods attached to wall-mounting brackets. The affected models include SBS-101BN, SBS-101PN, SBS-101PC, and SBS-101ORB, featuring finishes in brushed nickel, polished nickel, polished chrome, or oil-rubbed bronze. These seats measure 20 inches wide and 13 inches deep.
recalls.Title: Steamist Recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats Due to Fall and Laceration Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers should contact Steamist at 866-968-2250 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@steamist.com, or visit www.steamist.com/recall-notices. More information can also be found by clicking on 'Recall Notices' at the bottom of the Steamist website.

Products.Name: Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 3,900 (In addition, about 733 were sold in Canada and about 126 were sold in Mexico)

Images.Caption: Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with brushed nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished nickel finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with polished chrome finish
Recalled Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seat with oil-rubbed bronze finish

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 62 reports of the shower seats breaking, including 4 reports of injuries.

Retailers.Name: Available at specialty plumbing supply stores across the U.S., Canada, and Mexico from June 2009 to February 2015, priced between $490 and $660.

Importers.Name: Steamist Inc., of East Rutherford, New Jersey

Hazards.Name: Corrosion and breakage of wall mounting brackets and seat rods leading to fall and laceration risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers should cease use of the recalled shower seats immediately and reach out to Steamist to check if their product is affected and to receive recall participation instructions.

recalls.metaDescription: Steamist recalls Tilt-Up Bath & Shower Seats due to fall and laceration hazards. Check your product now for safety.
